the number of practice sessions per month that maximizes the team's average batting score, $ S $, is found by taking the derivative of the equation $ S = 50 + 5n - 0.2n^2 $ and setting it to zero. the derivative is $ \frac{dS}{dn} = 5 - 0.4n $. setting this equal to zero gives $ 5 - 0.4n = 0 $, which solves to $ n = 12.5 $. this is the optimal number of practice sessions. the maximum average batting score is achieved when $ n = 12.5 $, and substituting this value into the equation gives $ S = 50 + 5(12.5) - 0.2(12.5)^2 = 50 + 62.5 - 31.25 = 81.25 $.  

the team's win probability, $ P $, is given by $ P = \frac{1}{1 + e^{-(0.1S - 3)}} $. when $ n = 5 $, the average batting score is $ S = 50 + 5(5) - 0.2(5)^2 = 50 + 25 - 5 = 70 $. substituting this into the logistic function gives $ P = \frac{1}{1 + e^{-(0.1(70) - 3)}} = \frac{1}{1 + e^{-4}} \approx 0.9817 $. when $ n = 10 $, the average batting score is $ S = 50 + 5(10) - 0.2(10)^2 = 50 + 50 - 20 = 80 $. substituting this into the logistic function gives $ P = \frac{1}{1 + e^{-(0.1(80) - 3)}} = \frac{1}{1 + e^{-5.7}} \approx 0.9993 $. the change in win probability is $ 0.9993 - 0.9817 = 0.0176 $. the health of the team improves as they gain more practice sessions, which helps in both batting and bowling. the letter t appears in the sentence "the health of
